# Feature: Auto sufficiency requires ordered flows

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
Dogfood browo-hr 0.1.6: `scan --auto` marked **sufficient** via `interactive_coverage` (500+ source facts) while `orderedFlowCount === 0`, so crawl was skipped. Interactive facts alone must not skip crawl — escalate when `orderedFlowCount < minOrderedFlows` even if interactive coverage is high.

## Preconditions
- Core sufficiency module and scan `--auto` orchestrator exist (#144/#146)
- No browo-specific hardcodes

## Happy Path
- [ ] Many interactive facts/pages + 0 ordered flows → `evaluateSufficiency` returns `escalate` with DE reason (`missing_ordered_flows`)
- [ ] `scan --auto` on such evidence runs crawl (no „Evidenz bereits ausreichend — Crawl übersprungen“)
- [ ] ≥1 ordered flow alone → `sufficient`
- [ ] Empty scan → `blocked`
- [ ] Unit + integration coverage for browo-like false-positive
- [ ] No browo-specific hardcodes

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Interactive coverage still reported in reasons when sufficient via ordered flows
- [ ] Pages + few interactive facts + 0 flows → escalate (unchanged)

## Regression
- [ ] Existing scan/doctor tests still pass
- [ ] Explicit `--crawl` path unchanged

## Assumptions
- YAGNI: require ordered flows for `sufficient` by default (no new config knob yet)

## Screenshots
| Step | Filename |
|------|----------|
| n/a | CLI-only |

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/core/src/sufficiency/evaluate.ts` — `sufficient` only when `orderedFlowCount >= minOrderedFlows`; interactive-only → `escalate`
- `packages/core/src/sufficiency/types.ts` — criteria docs updated
- Tests: browo-like false-positive unit case; `scan-auto.integration.test.ts` asserts crawl not skipped
- No config knob yet (YAGNI)
